sentence: This book is more interesting than that one
explanation: For long adjectives (3+ syllables), use 'more...than': 'more interesting than'.

sentence: She is taller than her brother
explanation: Short adjectives add -er: 'taller than'. Don't use 'more' with short adjectives.

sentence: This is the best restaurant in the city
explanation: 'Good' has an irregular superlative: 'the best'. Always use 'the' before superlatives.

sentence: My house is bigger than yours
explanation: 'Big' is short: bigger (not 'more big'). Double the consonant: big → bigger.

sentence: English is easier than Chinese
explanation: 'Easy' ends in -y: change to -ier. 'Easier', not 'more easy'.

sentence: He is the tallest student in the class
explanation: Superlative of short adjectives: 'the tallest'. Always with 'the'.

sentence: Summer is hotter than spring
explanation: 'Hot' is short: hotter (not 'more hot'). Double consonant: hot → hotter.

sentence: This is the most expensive phone in the store
explanation: Superlative of long adjectives: 'the most expensive'.

sentence: Cats are smaller than dogs
explanation: Comparative with plural subjects uses 'are': 'Cats are smaller than dogs'.

sentence: This movie is worse than the last one
explanation: 'Bad' is irregular: worse (not 'badder').

sentence: She runs faster than her friends
explanation: Comparative adverbs also use -er for short words: 'faster than'.

sentence: This is the cheapest hotel near the beach
explanation: Superlative: 'the cheapest'. Place description at the end.

sentence: The blue car is more comfortable than the red one
explanation: 'Comfortable' is long: 'more comfortable than'.

sentence: He is the youngest person in the office
explanation: 'Young' → 'the youngest'. Change -g to -gest.

sentence: Today is colder than yesterday
explanation: 'Cold' is short: 'colder' (not 'more cold').

sentence: This exercise is the most difficult of all
explanation: Superlative of long adjective: 'the most difficult'. 'Of all' at the end.

sentence: My sister is older than me
explanation: 'Old' is short: 'older' (not 'more old').

sentence: This is the longest river in the country
explanation: Superlative: 'the longest'. 'In the country' specifies the group.

sentence: Learning online is more convenient than going to class
explanation: Gerund as subject + comparative of long adjective + than + gerund phrase.

sentence: He is the best player on the team
explanation: 'Good' → 'the best' (irregular). Never 'goodest'.
